meta.title: A3 — Constraint Engineering: Full Session Report
meta.description: April 10-12: 21 artifacts, 13 parity entries, 4 nouns frozen, overlay model, taxonomy soup, 18 checkpoints. With feedback sections for ambiguous decisions.

props.title: Constraint Engineering: The Full Picture
props.subtitle: 3 days, 18 checkpoints, 21 artifacts shipped, 10 items need your feedback

props.content: A constraint is a flowmind definition. A harness is the tooling to run it. **Leviathan is a meta-harness.** This session produced the manifesto, the overlay model, the taxonomy overlay, 13 parity entries, 12 cohesified articles, and a 5-agent federation. Now we need your decisions on 10 ambiguous items before the next phase.

props.label: SHIPPED
props.title: 21 Artifacts Delivered
props.subtitle: Expand to see the full list

props.content:
**Documents**: Manifesto (905L) + Product doc (199L) + ConstraintDriver interface (.ts) + Taxonomy overlay (212L) + DNA overlay model + Overlay schema (310L)

**FlowMind Flows**: eval-harness (6 nodes + OOO convergence) + adversarial-review (5 nodes) + doc-refine-tick (autowiki)

**Parity Registry**: 13 entries (AXI, dev-browser, agent-browser, AgentFS, GitAgent, Ouroboros, OpenClaw, Hermes, OMX, OMC, OMO, clawhip, lev-self-parity)

**Content**: Case study article + 12 articles interlinked (43 cross-links) + 3-pass narrative cohesion (16 threads, 0 slop)

**Federation**: 5-agent detangle (A0-A4), 58 mail messages, nugget dumps, claims/counterclaims resolved

**DNA**: Four nouns frozen in vernacular + overlay.schema.yaml + index.yaml updated with authoring_overlays activity

props.label: COMPARISON
props.title: 5-Phase SDLC: Lev vs OOO vs OMX
props.subtitle: With actual usage data from skill invocations

props.content:
**Phase 1 SHAPE**: Lev has /interview (71/mo) but no mathematical readiness gate. OOO has ambiguity scoring (0.2 threshold). OMX has $deep-interview with live scoring (0.2455 to 0.159). **Other agent now landed 10 canonical lifecycle_verbs including research, prior_art, interview with ambiguity_gate.**

**Phase 2 PLAN**: Lev dominates with /work (182/mo), /arch (12/mo), deepen-plan flow. OOO has Seed + Double Diamond. OMX has $ralplan. **Canonical verb: /propose (produces dna.yaml + execution.yaml)**

**Phase 3 EXECUTE**: Lev has lev-ralph + exec-validate + codex-autoresearch (14/mo). OOO has PAL Router (1x/10x/30x). OMX has $team + $ralph. **Canonical verb: /exec (runs execution.yaml topology)**

**Phase 4 EVALUATE**: LEV WINS. eval-harness + cdo (20/mo) + tribunal (9/mo) + adversarial-review. Constraint engineering is Lev-only. **Canonical verbs: /qa (auto) + /accept (human)**

**Phase 5 EVOLVE**: Lev has convergence (ontology + statistical), cass (13/mo), handoffs. OOO has Wonder-Reflect cycle. **Canonical verb: /handoff (close→shape transition)**

props.label: ARCHITECTURE
props.title: The Four Nouns (Overlay Model)
props.subtitle: Plugins project influence via overlays. Tasks are fractal mini-roots.

props.title: Ontology
props.content:
What CAN exist. System invariants, entity types, lifecycle domains.

**Lives in**: dna/graph.yaml + dna/entities.yaml
**Scope**: System-wide (frozen)
**Authority**: Canonical invariant

props.title: DNA (was: Definition)
props.content:
What IS this specific thing. Intent, acceptance, local constraints.

**Lives in**: .lev/tasks/<id>/dna.yaml
**Scope**: Per-task (authored)
**Authority**: User intent

*Renamed from 'definition' to 'dna' per other agent's canonical lifecycle_dimensions.*

props.title: Execution
props.content:
HOW it gets carried out. Topology, validation chain, receipts.

**Lives in**: .lev/tasks/<id>/execution.yaml
**Scope**: Per-task (compiled)
**Authority**: Compiled from DNA + overlays

*Standards + architecture are execution subdomains, not siblings.*

props.title: Projection
props.content:
How it APPEARS. Lossy scoped views of graph state.

**Lives in**: plugins/projections/<name>/
**Scope**: Multiple per entity
**Authority**: Derived

props.label: GAPS
props.title: What's Missing
props.subtitle: Honest depth assessment: everything A3 added is L0-L1 (referenced + syntax). Nothing executable.

props.title: Overlay Compiler (L1 → L3)
props.content: overlay.schema.yaml exists but nothing reads it. Need TypeScript/Rust compiler that takes plugin overlays + task DNA and produces merged definition.yaml + execution.yaml. Without this, the model is documentation.

props.title: Validation Depth Reporting
props.content: lev validate shows PASS/FAIL without indicating depth (L0-L5). Users think L1 syntax pass = executable. Need: 'L1: syntax valid. To reach L3: implement compiler per FlowMind authoring guide.'

props.title: FlowMind Authoring Guide
props.content: Multiple authoring guides scattered across repo. Need consolidation into one canonical guide. Overlay schema hooks into this guide.

props.title: Four Nouns ↔ Lifecycle Dimensions
props.content: My four_nouns says 'definition'. Canonical lifecycle_dimensions says 'dna'. My 6 triggers superseded by 10 canonical verbs. Need reconciliation pass across all A3 files.

props.label: RECONCILIATION
props.title: What Changed While A3 Worked
props.subtitle: Other agent landed canonical terms. Here's what needs updating.

props.content:
**Canonical lifecycle_dimensions** (from other agent, now in vernacular.yaml):
- ontology → **aligned** with my ontology
- dna → **renames** my 'definition' (I used 'definition', they used 'dna')
- execution → **aligned**
- projection → **aligned**

**Canonical lifecycle_verbs** (10 verbs, supersedes my 6 triggers):
research, prior_art, interview, propose, capture, exec, qa, accept, handoff, work

**New concept: decision_point** with 4 outcomes: keep (→autowiki), monitor (→workshop), execute (→sdlc), reject (→circuit breaker)

All A3 files that reference 'definition' or my 6 triggers need updating.

items.title: What is the primary CLI command?
items.insight: Session explored: `lev ce`, `lev work`, `lev job`, `lev delivery`. Other agent landed 10 canonical verbs that may supersede all of these.
items.detail: The 10 verbs (research, prior_art, interview, propose, capture, exec, qa, accept, handoff, work) may mean the primary command is just `lev work` with sub-verbs, not a new `lev ce` command.

options.label: lev work (with sub-verbs)
options.desc: Keep /work as the router, 10 verbs are sub-commands

options.label: lev ce (constraint engineering)
options.desc: New command surface, /work is interactive alias

options.label: lev job (beads-native)
options.desc: Job-centric, resolve from bd

options.label: Something else
options.desc: Describe in handoff

items.title: Is the second noun 'definition' or 'dna'?
items.insight: I used 'definition' (what IS being proposed). Other agent used 'dna' (behavioral contract). Both are correct from different angles.
items.detail: The files would be at .lev/tasks/<id>/dna.yaml either way. The name 'dna' is more Lev-native. The name 'definition' is more framework-agnostic.

options.label: dna
options.desc: More Lev-native, matches dna/ directory convention

options.label: definition
options.desc: More framework-agnostic, clearer to newcomers

options.label: Both (aliases)
options.desc: dna is canonical, definition is alias in docs

items.title: Are the lifecycle transitions 'triggers' (6) or 'verbs' (10)?
items.insight: My 6 triggers: /capture, /crystallize, /commit, /prove, /reflect, /focus. Their 10 verbs: research, prior_art, interview, propose, capture, exec, qa, accept, handoff, work.
items.detail: The 10 verbs are more granular and map to existing skills. The 6 triggers are more architectural (phase transitions). Could be complementary: verbs are what agents invoke, triggers are what the system fires at phase boundaries.

options.label: 10 verbs win
options.desc: More complete, already mapped to skills

options.label: 6 triggers win
options.desc: Cleaner, more architectural

options.label: Both (verbs are user-facing, triggers are system events)
options.desc: Verbs = commands, triggers = internal lifecycle events

items.title: Are standards and architecture execution subdomains or peer dimensions?
items.insight: I placed them under execution. The other agent's lifecycle_dimensions doesn't mention them explicitly.
items.detail: Standards (engineering, accessibility, security) and architecture (hexagonal, ui_component) feel like execution-time overlays — they influence HOW, not WHAT. But some argue they're closer to ontology (they define what valid structure looks like).

options.label: Execution subdomains
options.desc: Standards + architecture are HOW concerns

options.label: Peer dimensions
options.desc: Standards is its own dimension alongside ontology/dna/execution/projection

options.label: Overlay targets (neither)
options.desc: They're overlay classes, not dimensions

items.title: Should the overlay compiler be built now or after lifecycle verbs stabilize?
items.insight: The overlay schema exists (L1). Making it executable (L3) requires a compiler. But the lifecycle model is still evolving.

options.label: Build now (prove the model)
options.desc: Pick one task, compile overlays, show it works

options.label: Wait for lifecycle to stabilize
options.desc: The 10 verbs + decision_point are still fresh

options.label: Build a minimal POC (no full compiler)
options.desc: Script that reads 2 overlay YAMLs and merges them

items.title: Where do task-level DNA files live?
items.insight: I proposed dna/tasks/<id>/. Other agent proposed .lev/tasks/<id>/. Both have precedent.

options.label: .lev/tasks/<id>/
options.desc: Project-local, alongside .lev/pm/

options.label: dna/tasks/<id>/
options.desc: Under dna/ with other DNA files

options.label: Inline in beads
options.desc: No separate directory, task DNA lives in bead metadata

items.title: Do plugins get an overlays/ directory?
items.insight: I proposed plugins/<plugin>/overlays/{definition,execution,projection}/. No plugin has this today.

options.label: Yes, explicit overlays/ dir
options.desc: Clean separation of plugin logic from overlay declarations

options.label: No, overlays in config.yaml
options.desc: Keep it in existing config.yaml with an overlays: section

options.label: Deferred
options.desc: Don't add until the compiler exists

items.title: Should lev validate report depth levels (L0-L5)?
items.insight: Currently shows PASS/FAIL. I admitted L0-L1 depth for all my work. Users deserve to know what 'PASS' actually means.

options.label: Yes, show depth per check
options.desc: L1: syntax, L2: schema, L3: executable, L4: proven, L5: enforced

options.label: No, PASS/FAIL is enough
options.desc: Keep it simple

options.label: Yes but only for DNA files
options.desc: Only show depth for .dna.yaml and .flow.yaml, not general files

items.title: Fix security P0s in this workstream or separate?
items.insight: 4 critical security issues (shell injection, env leak, cipher, eval) blocking npm publish. Filed as lev-3bthw and lev-jy2cm. Untouched since April 10.

options.label: Fix in this workstream
options.desc: Proves constraint engineering on real security issues

options.label: Separate workstream
options.desc: Security fixes shouldn't be gated on taxonomy work

options.label: Deferred
options.desc: Not blocking constraint engineering work directly

items.title: Update manifesto to canonical 10 verbs or wait?
items.insight: Manifesto references old naming (shape/crystallize/manifest/deliver/review). Canonical lifecycle_verbs are now in vernacular. Update is mechanical but touches 1062 lines.

options.label: Update now
options.desc: Align before more people read it

options.label: Wait for lifecycle to stabilize
options.desc: Verbs might change again

options.label: Add a note, don't rewrite
options.desc: Prepend 'NOTE: naming updated, see vernacular.yaml lifecycle_verbs'

props.label: NEXT
props.title: Priority Queue
props.subtitle: Based on /capture results and feedback needed

props.title: 1. Reconcile four_nouns (P1)
props.content: lev-lchbr — definition→dna rename across all A3 files. Blocked on feedback item 'definition-vs-dna'.

props.title: 2. Validation depth reporting (P2)
props.content: lev-sx9pc — Make lev validate show L0-L5 depth. Blocked on feedback item 'validation-depth'.

props.title: 3. FlowMind authoring guide (P2)
props.content: lev-2fzrt — Find all authoring guides, consolidate, hook overlay schema into it.

props.title: 4. Overlay compiler POC (P1)
props.content: Minimal script that reads 2 overlay YAMLs + task DNA and merges them. Proves the model. Blocked on feedback items 'overlay-compiler-priority' and 'task-directory-location'.

props.title: 5. Security P0 fixes (P0)
props.content: lev-3bthw + lev-jy2cm — 4 critical issues. Blocked on feedback item 'security-p0s' (this workstream or separate?).
